signals.idea: Refactor ai-timeline-agent edge function from 840-line monolith to clean modular structure: split into loop/tools/llm/session modules, unified tool interface, remove dead Llama fallback code, fix message_user duplication, consistent error handling

unresolved_flags.concern: Agent chat rendering: Removing the duplicate assistant turn without a paired frontend change will hide `message_user` and `done` text inside the tool-call accordion instead of rendering it as a normal agent reply.
Tool error handling: Step 7's registry-wide catch would change current fatal timeline/DB failures into ordinary tool results, which is a behavior change outside the requested session-tool error fix.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 4.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.

items: `index.ts` is under 80 lines — only HTTP handling and delegation
`deno check` (or equivalent) passes with no type errors across all new modules
The Llama fallback regex and parser block are completely removed
All tools dispatch through a single `TOOL_REGISTRY` lookup — no if/else chain for tool names
The `SupabaseAdmin` type is defined once in `types.ts` and used everywhere — no hand-typed inline interfaces
`createGenerationTask` returns error results instead of throwing on HTTP failures
`message_user` and `done` no longer create duplicate turns (tool_result + assistant with same content)
The function deploys successfully and passes a manual smoke test (send message, receive response, tool calls work)
The turns array format in `timeline_agent_sessions` is unchanged except for the removed duplicate assistant turns
No new features or behavior changes beyond the 4 specified fixes (Llama removal, duplicate turns, error handling, dispatch cleanup)

unresolved_flags.concern: Agent chat rendering: Removing the duplicate assistant turn without a paired frontend change will hide `message_user` and `done` text inside the tool-call accordion instead of rendering it as a normal agent reply.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The current UI skips every `tool_result` turn and only keeps it as the paired result inside a grouped tool accordion (`src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:41-53`). The existing dedupe only suppresses the extra assistant turn when it exactly matches the preceding `message_user` tool result (`src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:58-70`). If step 8 removes the backend duplicate at `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts:779-781` without updating the renderer, the agent's conversational text stops appearing as a normal chat message.

unresolved_flags.concern: Tool error handling: Step 7's registry-wide catch would change current fatal timeline/DB failures into ordinary tool results, which is a behavior change outside the requested session-tool error fix.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Today, timeline load/save helpers throw on DB failures (`supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts:327-365,396-423`), and the outer handler records an internal-error turn, marks the session `error`, and returns HTTP 500 (`supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts:818-846`). The requested behavior change was limited to session tools returning errors instead of throwing, but Step 7 proposes catching thrown errors inside `executeTool` and converting them to `{ result: "Tool error: ..." }`, which would silently downgrade real persistence failures.
